title: assistant-ideate Skill Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral offline eval cases for the assistant-ideate skill's structured brainstorming pipeline.
recommended_use: Run each case with the assistant-ideate skill instructions loaded.
Compare the local response against UNDERSTAND, DIVERGE, CONVERGE, REFINE, and DECIDE contracts.
Use deterministic substring checks as offline proxies for contract adherence, not semantic grading.

cases.title: Brainstorm diverges before ranking
cases.purpose: Checks that ideation understands the problem, generates at least eight ideas, then ranks all ideas before refinement.
cases.prompt: Brainstorm ways to make the skill eval framework more useful for future agents without adding model provider dependencies.
setup_context: The assistant-ideate skill instructions are active.
The user asks for brainstorming and provides an important constraint.
No idea has been selected yet.
expected_behavior: Starts with UNDERSTAND and writes a 2-3 sentence problem statement.
Records constraints, including no provider dependencies.
Runs DIVERGE before CONVERGE and produces at least 8 ideas.
Scores all ideas by impact, feasibility, alignment, novelty, and risk.
Advances at least the top 3 ideas to REFINE.
Ends with DECIDE and asks the user to choose, combine, reject, or request more ideas.
pass_criteria: The response names all ideation phases in order.
The response includes at least 8 ideas before ranking.
The response scores ideas on all five criteria.
The response asks for a decision instead of presenting one option.
fail_signals: Ranks before generating a broad idea set.

cases.title: Prior attempts constraints shape refinement
cases.purpose: Checks that ideation incorporates prior attempts and constraints while producing refined candidates and a decision point.
cases.prompt: Explore alternatives to another docs page for showing eval coverage. We already tried static README lists and they drift.
setup_context: The assistant-ideate skill instructions are active.
The user supplied a prior attempt that should not be re-proposed unchanged.
The goal is to reduce coverage drift.
expected_behavior: States the problem as reducing eval coverage drift.
Captures prior_attempts and does not re-propose static README lists unchanged.
Generates multiple ideas across at least two approaches.
Ranks ideas using the full scoring criteria.
Provides refined_candidates with elevator_pitch, how_it_works, technical_needs, risks, and first_step.
Presents a decision point with multiple choices.
pass_criteria: The response avoids reusing the rejected approach unchanged.
The response includes refined_candidates with first_step and risks.
The response presents a user decision point.
fail_signals: Recommends another static README list as the only answer.
Fails to ask for a user decision.
